name: Hungarian Stuffed Red Bell Peppers
recipeIngredient: 2 Tablespoons Freshly Rendered Lard or Oil
1 lb Finely Ground Pork, Veal, or Chicken
Salt and Freshly Ground Pepper to Taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the very top part of the peppers. Chop the pepper tops finely, omitting the stems, and save for the filling.
2. Seed and core the peppers; set aside.
3. Heat a large covered frying pan and add the lard or oil.
4. Saute the garlic and onion until tender.
5. Add the reserved chopped pepper tops and cook for a few minutes.
6. Stir in the parsley and rice, then saute for a few more minutes.
7. Gradually add 1/2 cup of chicken stock; cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
8. In a large bowl, combine the ground meat, paprika, egg, salt, and pepper-rice mixture.
9. Fill each pepper with the meat mixture, leaving about an inch at the top.
10. Place the filled peppers in a Dutch oven.
11. Add the remaining chicken stock to the bottom of the pot.
12. Cover and simmer very gently for 45 minutes, keeping the temperature low or the peppers will break.
13. Gently move the peppers around during cooking to prevent sticking.
14. Pour the paprika gravy over the peppers; cover and simmer for an additional 20 minutes.
Note: The recipe for paprika gravy is shared by Sheryl Donner in REG4.
